quiz.title: AP Calculus AB Quiz 12
quiz.subtitle: Applications of Derivatives — Motion, Rates, and Linear Approximation (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Calculus

questions.question: The position of a particle moving along a line is given by $s(t) = \ln(t^2 + 1) - 2t$, where $s$ is in feet and $t$ is in seconds ($t \geq 0$). Find the velocity of the particle at $t = 1$ second.
Find the slope of the tangent line to the curve $f(x) = \arctan x$ at the point where $x = 1$.

A rectangular prism has a square base. The side length of the base is increasing at a rate of 0.2 cm/s, while the height is decreasing at a rate of 0.5 cm/s. How fast is the volume changing when the base side is 6 cm and the height is 10 cm? (Volume $V = s^2 h$)
The profit (in hundreds of dollars) from selling $x$ thousand units is given by $P(x) = x \ln x - 2x$. Find the marginal profit when $x = 5$ thousand units.
questions.explanation: Marginal profit $P'(x) = \ln x - 1$. At $x = 5$: $P'(5) = \ln 5 - 1 \approx 1.6094 - 1 = 0.6094$ hundred dollars per thousand units (approximately $60.94 per 1000 units).

questions.question: A particle moves along a line with position $s(t) = 4\sqrt{t} - t$, where $t \geq 0$. Find the velocity at $t = 4$ seconds. When does the particle come to rest?
questions.explanation: Velocity $v(t) = \frac{2}{\sqrt{t}} - 1$. At $t = 4$: $v(4) = \frac{2}{2} - 1 = 0$ ft/s. Particle is at rest when $v(t) = 0$: $t = 4$ seconds.

questions.question: Find the equation of the tangent line to $f(x) = e^{-x^2}$ at the point $(0, 1)$. Use it to estimate the value of $e^{-0.04}$.
questions.explanation: $f'(x) = -2x e^{-x^2}$, slope at $x = 0$ is 0. Tangent: $y = 1$. Estimate: $e^{-0.04} \approx 1$. (Actual value is approximately 0.9608 — horizontal tangent gives constant approximation.)

questions.question: Using the position function $s(t) = \ln(t^2 + 1) - 2t$, find the acceleration of the particle at $t = 2$ seconds.
The current in a circuit (in amperes) is given by $I(t) = 5\sin(2t) + 3$, where $t$ is in seconds. Find the instantaneous rate of change of current at $t = \frac{\pi}{4}$ seconds.
A kite is flying at a height of 40 feet and moving horizontally away from the person flying it at a rate of 5 ft/s. How fast is the string being let out when the string length is 50 feet?
questions.explanation: Given $x^2 + 40^2 = L^2$. When $L = 50$, $x = 30$. Differentiate: $x\frac{dx}{dt} = L\frac{dL}{dt}$. $30(5) = 50\frac{dL}{dt}$ → $\frac{dL}{dt} = 3$ ft/s.

questions.question: The graph of $f(x)$ has a vertical asymptote at $x = 2$ and a horizontal tangent at $x = -1$. What can you say about $f'(2)$ and $f'(-1)$?
questions.explanation: $f'(2)$ is undefined (vertical asymptote means function is not continuous/differentiable at $x = 2$). $f'(-1) = 0$ (horizontal tangent indicates derivative is zero).

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered applications of derivatives.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand most derivative applications.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on setting up problems correctly.
scoreMessages.0: Review basic differentiation rules and their applications.
